rulings.Case Title: ALICEA VS GOLDEN EAGLE INSURANCE CORP
rulings.Rulings: Defendant's (GOLDEN EAGLE) Motion for SUMMARY JUDGMENT/SUMMARY ADJUDICATION on Plaintiff's Complaint: DENY SUMMARY JUDGMENT AND GRANT SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IN PART. DENY SUMMARY ADJUDICATION ON 1ST CAUSE OF ACTION [BAD FAITH]. TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT RE MATERIALITY, WAIVER AND REASONABLENESS OF DEFENDANT'S DELAY. GRANT SUMMARY ADJUDICATION ON 2ND CAUSE OF ACTION [NEG.]. NO EVIDENCE THAT MIRANDA WAS GOLDEN EAGLE'S AGENT. GRANT SUMMARY ADJUDICATION ON 3RD CAUSE OF ACTION [DEC. RELIEF]. NO EVIDENCE THAT MIRANDA WAS GOLDEN EAGLE'S AGENT; NO BASIS TO FIND THAT PLAINTIFF OBTAINED COVERAGE OTHER THAN COVERAGE SPECIFIED IN WRITTEN POLICY. DENY SUMMARY ADJUDICATION AS TO PUNITIVE DAMAGES. TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT RE MALICE. Defendant's (GOLDEN EAGLE) Motion for SUMMARY ADJUDICATION on X-COMPLAINT: GRANT AS TO EQUITABLE AND CONTRACTUAL INDEMNITY. X-DEFENDANT (LA JOLLA) FAILED TO ESTABLISH THAT GOLDEN EAGLE WAS SOLELY RESPONSIBLE FOR ACTS OF BAD FAITH ALLEGED BY PLAINTIFF. See Continental Heller v. Amtech Mechanical Services, Inc., (1997) 53 Cal.App.4th 500. (PB)
